The Fender® Vintera III series delivers meticulously crafted vintage recreations that capture the authentic look, feel, and tone of iconic Fender guitars from the '50s, '60s, and '70s.

The Vintera® III Early '70s Jazz Bass® features an alder body and maple neck with maple or rosewood fingerboard for classic Fender tone that's full of punch and clarity. The early '70s "C"-shape maple neck sports binding and block inlays with 7.25" radius maple or rosewood fingerboard and vintage-tall frets. At its heart, you'll find a pair of vintage-style early '70s single-coil pickups that deliver all the punchy and articulate, warm and woody tone that made Fender® famous. Other features include a vintage-style 4-saddle bridge for rock-solid intonation and sustain and vintage-style tuning machines for enhanced tuning stability.

Embrace the authentic vintage spirit and legendary sound of Vintera® III – where every detail tells the story.

item.title: Dingwall SP1 Gloss Dark Candy Green — $1,917.84
item.description:
$1,917.84 · Brand New · Oxbow Audio Lab

The SP1 is passive, so there are no batteries or EQ boost, but the quality of the tonewoods, alnico-loaded pickups, and the overall construction mean that theres absolutely no need for them. The front control is volume, and the back is a master tone.

The Dingwall Minimalist bridge features a custom riser system thats designed to provide an increased range of flexibility when it comes to adjusting the action. If you play with a heavy technique, instead of raising your individual bridge saddles, which can put stress on the string and cause premature breakage, you can raise the entire bridge unit. That way you can move between a high action or a low action just by adjusting the shims, which leaves the saddles only for fine tuning. The tuners are lightweight, open-gear designs, and there is also a bullet string retainer to help maintain a decent break angle across the Graphtech nut.

Dingwall strings have been meticulously tested to maximize performance and deliver a tone that blends effortlessly with any style of music. We were the first company to design strings specifically for multi-scale instruments. Available in both nickel and stainless steel, our strings provide an even tension for optimal balance, letting you experience the warmest highs and the tightest lows possible.

item.title: Sadowsky Guitars MetroExpress 21-Fret Hybrid P/J 4-String Bass with Gigbag - Ocean Blue Metallic — $881.03
item.description:
$881.03 · Brand New · Oxbow Audio Lab

Roger Sadowsky's goal has always been to improve on bass design by making some very unique and important enhancements. Through the use of his proprietary special electronics, light-weight tuners and selected tone wood, Roger has raised this MetroExpress 21-Fret Hybrid P/J 4-String Bass to a whole other level! Features include a light-weight solid Okoume body finished in Solid Ocean Blue Metallic High Polish with a parchment pickguard, a roasted maple neck and a roasted maple fingerboard, this is one fantastic looking and playing bass that we are sure you will love to play! Sadowsky's MetroExpress basses are the easiest way for you to access that famous Sadowsky tone: Never harsh or shrill sounding, with strong, powerful but never muddy bass, with present, but not too aggressive mids, and a sweet, airy treble.

Specifications

- Model: Sadowsky MetroExpress 21-Fret Hybrid P/J Bass
- Number of Strings: 4-String
- Orientation: Right-Hand
- Scale Length: 34" (864 mm) Long Scale
- Body Shape: Sleek undersized J-Style body
- Body Wood: Okoume, Solidbody
- Neck Wood: Roasted Maple (C-Shape, Bolt-On)
- Fingerboard Wood: Roasted Maple
- Inlays: White dot inlays
- Fingerboard Radius: 12"
- Nut: Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III
- Nut Width: 1.5" (38.5 mm)
- Nut String Spacing: Nut string spacing (all strings): 1.22" / 31 mm
- Neck width at 12th fret: 2.22" / 56.3 mm neck width at 12th fret
- Neck width at last fret: 2.59" / 65.7 mm neck width at last fret
- Neck thickness at 1st fret: 0.82" / 20.8 mm neck thickness at 1st fret
- Neck thickness at 12th fret: 0.96" / 24.6 mm neck thickness at 12th fret
- Number of Frets: 21 Nickel Silver (width: 0.094" (2.4 mm) / height: 0.051" (1.3 mm))
- Neck Pickup: Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup
- Bridge Pickup: Sadowsky passive hum-cancelling J-Style pickup in 60s position
- Preamp: Active Sadowsky 2-way electronics (Bass and Treble boost)
- Controls: Volume / Balance / Treble / Bass
- Push/Pull Function: Push/pull function at Bass knob for preamp bypass
- Machine Heads: Sadowsky Light Open Gear Machine Heads
- Security Locks: Sadowsky S-Security Locks
- Bridge: Sadowsky bridge with quick string release
- Bridge Total String Spacing: 2.24" / 57 mm
- Hardware Color: Chrome
- Strings: Sadowsky Silver Label, Stainless Steel, 045 - 105
- Finish: Solid Ocean Blue Metallic High Polish
- Pickguard: Parchment
- Weight: Approx. 9.5lbs / 4.3kg (can vary)
- Protector: Sadowsky Fretboard Protector
- Tools: Hex key tools
- Bag / Case: Sadowsky PortaBag

item.description:

Here's a really cool Jackson mini bass. Scale length is just under 30 inches, so the fret spacing is closer to a guitar. Tons of fun to play. P/J pickup setup. Very lightweight.

Very cool bright green glossy finish, which is no longer offered.

Matching custom gig bag with the Jackson logo.

This would be great for beginners with small hands or for players who are mainly guitarists and want to play bass from time to time. Also a great travel bass due to the compact size.

Thanks for looking!

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass Solid Body Electric Bass Guitar (1960), ser. #54837, original brown tolex hard shell case. — $19,500
item.description:
$19,500 · Excellent · Retrofret Vintage Guitars

Fender Jazz Bass Model Solid Body Electric Bass Guitar (1960), made in Fullerton, California, serial # 54837, sunburst lacquer re- finish, alder body, maple neck with rosewood fingerboard, original brown tolex hard shell case.

Sometimes superlatives fail, and you just fall back on "it is what it is". What THIS is, is a simply wonderful-feeling and sounding Fender Jazz bass, one of the finest-playing electric basses we have had. This particular early "stack-knob" Jazz Bass has had an absolutely top notch restoration including an expert nitro refinish by J. Black, former Fender Custom Shop Master Builder and one of the world's foremost experts on the restoration of old Fenders. These first Jazz basses have long been considered the cream of Fender's 4-string output, and playing this one it is easy to see why.

The introduction of the Jazz Bass represented a major step both for Fender and the electric bass in general. By 1959 the Precision Bass had been on the market for eight years, and Fender saw increasing competition in the 4-string market they had created. The Jazz Bass was designed to be the upgrade model, the hot rod of the electric bass world. With the sleeker "offset" body adapted from the Jazzmaster and a much slimmer 1 1/2" nut width, it had a racier look and feel than the Precision. The two slimmer-cased, multi-pole pickups offered a wider range of sounds, with a sharper attack and more definition to the tone.

The new model was officially introduced in summer 1960, although the first samples had already been shown in March that year. This bass' neck and body are marked 9-60 in pencil, which is when the first quantity production of the new model got under way. Typically Fender sales would show samples to dealers at the summer NAMM show, take orders, and then tool up to produce what the dealers were expecting in the fall. This bass carries early examples of the two-on-one pots that give the "stack-knob" its nickname, with concentric knobs mounted on one shaft. This arrangement allows individual tone and volume controls to be offered on a relatively small control plate, and was pioneered by Danelectro (hey, even Leo borrowed ideas!). The pots on this bass are stamped to the 39th week of 1960.

This bass also has all the other signature hallmarks the characteristic of the first-year "stack-knob", including (under the tailpiece cover) the spot where four individual string mutes were once mounted, and an outside metal grounding strip running from the pickup to the bridge. The alder body carries a perfectly executed sunburst refinish; it actually looks pretty much original even on close inspection. The pickguard is a very light and attractive "pepperoni colored" tortoise celluloid, typical of this period. The large chrome coverplates are intact; the rear chrome cover is original and the forward is an reissue.

This bass weighs in at 9.90 lbs. and is perfectly balanced, handles beautifully, and has a very well-defined, singing sound with plenty of character. The clay dot, slab rosewood fingerboard neck is very slim and exquisitely comfortable. This is one of the finest Fender bass restorations we can imagine and carries a serious vibe for any Jaco fan, vintage-obsessed bass player, or classic Fender bass connoisseur. Fender's introductory promo sheet claimed the instrument "represents the standards to which others will be compared" and for once even the ad copywriter was understating the case!

Overall length is 46 in. (116.8 cm.), 14 in. (35.6 cm.) wide at lower bout, and 1 5/8 in. (4.1 cm.) in depth, measured at side of rim. Scale length is 34 in. (864 mm.). Width of nut is 1 1/2 in. (38 mm.).

This bass had a complete restoration some time ago to the original specs by one of the world's best Fender Master builder/restorers, J. Black. The finish is a beautifully recreated and lightly aged 3-color sunburst on the body only; the back of the neck is the original finish somewhat worn down with some very light overspray. The headstock is the original finish and decal; there is a decent sized spot of the Fender logo flaked away. The body finish has a very light checking pattern and is not heavily "reliced" but has some small worn through spots mostly on the edges. The body itself has no additional routs or other repair work other than the refinish.

Most of the hardware is original to the instrument, including the all-important 1960 pickups, plastic pickup covers and wiring, internal shielding plates, control plate and "stacked" knobs, pickguard, tuners, fingerrest, bridge cover, neckplate and neck screws. A ground wire was added from the jack to the rearmost pot in the control cavity. Non-original parts are the center chrome cover and its screws, the pickguard screws and the pan-head screws retaining the chrome bridge cover. One corner is chipped off the lower tip of the pickguard, which has some typical shrinkage but it otherwise intact. The plating on the chrome covers and tuners shows a decent amount of wear. The mute pieces once mounted in front of the bridge are long gone but the original external grounding strip is still in place.

The original small thin wire frets have been dressed down a bit but remain in good playing condition; the nut is a replacement. This is a wonderful player's instrument that retains its original very articulate sound and superb playing characteristics with an appearance that as about close to original as it is possible for a refinished instrument to get. With some play wear this bass will "relic" itself as years go on! It also includes a well-worn original Brown Tolex hard case, showing moisture wear internally but not odorous; only the center latch is still working. Restored to Excellent Condition.

item.title: Fender - 2023 American Vintage II '54 Precision Bass Electric Bass 2 Tone Sunburst w/OHSC - USED — $1,899.99
item.description:
$1,899.99 · Very Good · Manchester Music Mill

Fender - 2023 American Vintage II '54 Precision Bass Electric Bass 2 Tone Sunburst w/OHSC - USED (MMM122523)

8 pounds, 1 ounce.

This 2023 Fender American Vintage II '54 Precision Bass beautifully captures the essence of a foundational era in electric bass history. Featuring a striking 2-Tone Sunburst finish over a resonant body, this instrument reproduces the authentic look, feel, and tone of the original mid-50s icons. It features a period-correct thick "U"-shaped maple neck, a 7.25" radius fingerboard, and vintage-style frets that offer a classic playing experience. The single-coil '54 Precision Bass pickup delivers the unmistakable, organic punch and clear low-end definition that cemented the P-bass as a studio and stage legend.

This particular instrument comes in used condition, weighing in at a comfortable 8 pounds, 1 ounce. It has been well-maintained and retains its classic aesthetic and stellar playability. Perfect for players and collectors alike who crave pure vintage Fender vibe without the vintage price tag, it comes complete with its Original Hard Shell Case (OHSC) for secure transport and storage.

item.title: Fender Squier Classic Vibe '60s Jazz Bass, Tahitian Coral — $529.99
item.description:
$529.99 · Brand New · Music Land

A tribute to the decade of its birth, the Classic Vibe '60s Jazz Bass® combines the luxurious playability that made it famous with the versatility and massive tone of its dual Fender-Designed alnico single-coil pickups. Player-friendly features include a slim, comfortable "C"-shaped neck profile with an easy-playing 9.5"-radius fingerboard and narrow-tall frets, as well as a vintage-style bridge with threaded saddles. This throwback Squier model also features 1960s-inspired headstock markings, rich-looking nickel-plated hardware and a slick vintage-tint gloss neck finish for an old-school vibe.

From avant-garde to southern soul to funk and all notes in-between, MonoNeon's astounding musical range, tone and taste are always on full display. He collaborated with Prince, recorded Making a Movie with Ne-Yo, and won a Grammy for Best Rap Album with Nas. MonoNeon's massive YouTube channel showcases his mind-bending jams and masterpieces on his favorite Fender® Basses.

The MonoNeon Jazz Bass® V features an alder body finished in an eye-popping neon yellow urethane finish, complemented by a neon orange painted headstock and pickguard. The 22-fret one-piece roasted maple neck features a 10"-14" compound radius fingerboard and elegant white pearl block inlays. Custom-wound Fireball® 5-string Bass Humbucking pickups deliver fat, punchy tone while the 18v active preamp with 3-band EQ provides flexible tone-shaping for any style of music. The Fender HiMass™ string-through/top-load bridge provides increased resonance and sustain, and the gold hardware adds to the distinctive look of this stunning bass. Also Included are a MonoNeon sticker pack and custom headstock sock. The MonoNeon Jazz Bass V makes a statement - equal parts swagger and serious business.

Eye-Popping Neon Yellow and Orange Finish
Custom Fireball® 5-String Bass Humbucking Pickups

item.title: Epiphone SG Bass Regular Cherry — $303.75
item.description:
$303.75 · Fair · Music123

** Has a chip on the tip of the headstock.    Has a cracked neck at the nut to the 2nd fret.    Has scratches on the pickguard.     Missing the truss rod wrench.    Missing the paperwork. **

In the early 1960s the Kalamazoo factory, home to both Epiphone and Gibson instruments, decided to cease production of the Les Paul guitar. In its place came a radical new guitar design that also influenced the Kalamazoo factory's bass designs. With the Les Paul (temporarily) out of the picture, the SG took over. And for a time during that golden era of manufacturing, the classic SG bass was the historic factory's sole electric bass.

But the EB "SG" bass was by no means a cast-off. It quickly became one of the most attractive and distinctive basses in rock and players like Bill Wyman of The Rolling Stones, Cream's Jack Bruce and The Who's John Entwistle made rock history on SG-style basses. Now, Epiphone has created this modern SG Bass, a stunning recreation of the vintage marvel with all the tone and feel of the original without the vintage price tag and vintage problems.

item.title: Fender Custom Shop 62 Precision BASS - Rosewood Fingerboard - New Old Stock Finish - Lake Placid Blue - 8.8 pounds - R148500 — $3,039
item.description:
$3,039 · B-Stock · The Bass Place

This ’62 Precision Bass in Lake Placid Blue is a particularly strong example of the model. The AAA round-lam rosewood fingerboard is a deep, dark chocolate color, exactly what you hope to see on a great early-’60s P, and it pairs beautifully with the LPB finish.

Sonically, this one is exceptionally well balanced. The bass is lively and resonant, with a singing fundamental and an even response across the neck. Notes bloom easily, sustain naturally, and sit right where you want them—full without being wooly, clear without being stiff.

It’s been properly set up and plays effortlessly, making it one of those instruments that feels immediately familiar and comfortable. In our experience, this is a Goldilocks ’62 Precision Bass: all the right vintage features, none of the extremes, and an overall feel that just works.

NOTE *

We have found a couple of very minor imperfections: one smaller than a pinhead on the body—very hard to detect or photograph and visible only up close if the lighting is just right, and some minor string winder marks on the keys—the three photos provided show the difference in visibility under different lighting conditions. Hence, the deep discount on this otherwise great bass. (See Photos)

item.description:
$945 · Very Good · Olivia's Vintage

Olivia's Vintage would like to present this 1980's Fender Made in Japan Contemporary Power Jazz Bass Special in its original Candy Apple Red finish. It has a great playing neck with great frets. It's all original with the exception of the added bridge cover and thumbrest. A nice hardshell case is included. It's in very good condition with a few very minor nicks + dings and other minor cosmetic wear. The bass itself remains structurally sound with no cracks, breaks or repairs. All electronics work and sound great.
We are happy to ship internationally.

item.title: 1994 Orville by Gibson Thunderbird IV Reverse Body Bass Vintage Sunburst, Japan Terada — $1,599.99
item.description:
$1,599.99 · Very Good · Mike & Mike's Guitar Bar

Up for sale, a 1994 Orville by Gibson Thunderbird IV bass in 100% original condition and in perfect working order. Made exclusively for the Japanese market and uncommon outside its country of origin, this Orville is an official Gibson-licensed guitar and a result of a partnership between Gibson USA and their Japanese distributor Yamano Gakki. Named after Gibson's founder, Orville Gibson, this long-since discontinued Orville model was crafted at the Terada factory.

As its name would suggest, this bass thunders and growls with the best, producing incredible low end thump and power from the nickel-plated Gibson USA humbuckers. Extremely faithful to the Gibson Thunderbird IV of the early '60s, this bass features pickups wound to vintage spec, measuring 7.4k ohms in both positions and delivering excellent clarity, treble detail and punch. Weighing only 8lbs 5oz and strung with 45-105 roundwound strings, this Thunderbird IV has been professionally setup here at Mike & Mike's Guitar Bar with easy-playing action and accurate intonation.

Neck Specs:

  • Wood: Mahogany
  • Shape: Slender U, measuring .780” 1st fret, .955” 12th fret
  • Fretboard: Rosewood, pearloid dot inlays
  • Frets: Medium jumbo, no wear
  • Scale Length: 34”
  • Nut: 1 1/2”
  • Tuners: Clover buttons
  • Serialization: G4-prefix (1994)

Body Specs:

  • Wood: Mahogany
  • Pickups: Gibson USA Thunderbird humbucker x2
  • Harness: Full-size Noble pots, untouched solder
  • Controls: Volume x2, Tone
  • Hardware: Three-point bridge
  • Plastics: Reflector cap knobs, three-ply pickguard with Thunderbird graphic

Cosmetic wear on the gloss Vintage Sunburst finish includes some shallow wear along the bass side of the pick path, an area of minor buckle rash central to the back, a bit of chipping at the bass-side lower body point, and some lesser dings consistent with use. The gloss on the neck profile is smooth and clean with a few shallow marks along the shoulders.

A modern padded gigbag is included.

item.title: Fender Custom Shop 59 P Bass Special - New Old Stock - Shoreline Gold - 9.6 lbs - Serial # R148614 — $3,869
item.description:
$3,869 · Brand New · The Bass Place

*This bass weighs 9.3 lbs with the covers taken off

Finished in Shoreline Gold over an alder body, this ’59 P-Bass Special is all about authority—big, focused low end, a chunky neck, and unmistakable presence. It’s been sorted by the Custom Shop and is a bit heftier than others we’ve seen, but the payoff is immediate: rock-solid construction and muscular, no-nonsense tone. Think Steve Harris–style punch and clarity, with lows that stay tight and controlled no matter how hard you dig in.

This build features a factory P/J pickup configuration, a chunky ’59-profile neck with a 9.5" radius, 1.75" nut width, incredibly resonant and alive in the hands.

 It’s a versatile setup that still feels unmistakably “P-Bass first.”

The neck is the standout—big, bold, and sustaining. With 6105 frets, a tinted NOS finish, and a comfortable oval “C” shape, it has that old-school feel that instantly inspires confidence. Meaty paws welcome—dig in and this bass pays you back in spades.

Finished with chrome/nickel hardware, black dot inlays, vintage decal, and factory 45–105 strings. Built specifically for us here at The Bass Place.

A rare, player-first Custom Shop build with serious low-end authority and monster tone.

item.title: Longhorn bass custom 4 string Kahler Bridge + EMG active pickup Bill Foley gigbag — $895
item.description:
$895 · Very Good · Viking Woodsmith

I’ve owned this a couple of years. Haven’t played it much and figure I’ll let it go.

It’s a custom made long horn bass with a Kahler bass bridge + EMG active pickup by Bill Foley. I don’t know much about him but I bought it here on Reverb few years ago.

I can tell you the craftsmanship is top notch and the material used are high quality. The Kahler bridge alone goes for $150 new. The EMG active pickup is around $100 from what I can tell. The action is excellent. The strings are a wound round so they are smoother. Lots of life left in them. I can’t tell what kind of tuners these are but they hold true tuning very well. The paint job and black striping is really cool and nicely done.

It’s a short scale bass 30.5 inches from nut to saddles.

No noticeable fret ware.

Just a great bass guitar ready to rock. Let me know if you have questiobe, I can try and answer them. Feel free to shoot over an offer. I’ll probably regret selling it within a year!

Description
Step into the spotlight and experience iconic Fender® sound and style with the Player II Precision Bass® — a stage-ready bass guitar with contemporary updates to power your performance and inspire your playing.

The Player II Precision Bass radiates timeless Fender charm, but under the hood, it's primed for today's players. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets. A classic alder body is available in both timeless Fender finishes and never-before-seen colors unearthed from the archives. Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil P bass® pickups offer sweet highs, musical mids and seismic lows that elevate any genre. An adjustable 4-saddle bridge, single-groove steel saddles and open-gear tuners ensure precise tuning stability for the flexibility to explore endless sonic possibilities.

Perfect for crafting your own sound, the Player II Precision Bass has the looks, tone and feel that only a Fender delivers.

Features
Contoured Alder Body
Modern "C" Neck Profile
9.5“-Radius Maple or Rosewood Fingerboard with Rolled Edges
Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil Precision Bass® Pickups
4-Saddle Bridge with Single-Groove Steel “Barrel” Saddles
Open-Gear Tuning Machines

item.title: Gibson 2019 Thunderbird Electric Bass Guitar Heritage Cherry Sunburst with Hard Case — $2,599.99
item.description:
$2,599.99 · Excellent · Strait Music

This pre-owned Gibson Thunderbird features a rare cherry sunburst finish which was part of a limited run in 2019. It is in excellent condition overall with a small amount of belt buckle wear on the back of the body. A custom hand-tooled leather pickguard and the factory white pickguard are included.

The Gibson Thunderbird has the classic reverse body and headstock design as originally introduced in 1963 as Gibson's first neck-through-body bass design. The traditional 9-ply mahogany/walnut neck through body construction provides a thundering low end response and a piano like sustain. The narrow nut width and rounded neck profile neck feels both fast and intuitive. The Thunderbird high output, ceramic magnet loaded humbucking pickups (neck and bridge) deliver the sonic and iconic low end voice for which the Thunderbird is known.

Features:

  • Heritage Cherry Sunburst Finish
  • Mahogany Body
  • 9 Piece Mahogany/Walnut Through Neck
  • Rosewood Fretboard with 12" Radius
  • Acrylic Dot Inlays
  • 34" Scale Length
  • 1.50" Nut Width
  • Rounded Neck Profile
  • T-Bird Rhythm & Lead Humbuckers
  • Two Volume, Master Tone Controls
  • Three Point Bass Bridge with Adjustable Saddles
  • Grover Bass Tuners with 20:1 Ratio
  • Black Hardware

Gear up and get ready to take your performance to the next level with Player II Modified. Featuring key upgrades for the modern guitarist and bassist, these versatile instruments are designed to handle anything you can throw at them.

The Player II Modified Active Jazz Bass® is a classic bass with the modern player in mind. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or 1-piece maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets. The alder body provides classic Fender punch and is available in a variety of exciting new finishes. The Player II noiseless™ pickups deliver classic Jazz Bass® growl while eliminating unwanted noise while the all-new active preamp with passive tone control and EQ section gives players the perfect tool to dial in their favorite tones. The 4-saddle HiMass™ bridge, tapered-shaft tuners and TUSQ® nut all combine to keep tuning incredibly stable for even the most demanding performances.

Upgrade your arsenal today with the Player II Modified Active Jazz Bass®.

item.title: Ibanez Premium SR4A250 USA 250th Anniversary Electric Bass Guitar - Red, White, and Blue w/ Bag — $1,299.99
item.description:
$1,299.99 · Brand New · Victor Litz Music Center

Ibanez Premium SR4A250 Limited Edition 250th Anniversary Bass

Celebrate 250 years of American history with the Ibanez Premium SR4A250, a limited-edition bass that combines eye-catching patriotic styling with the exceptional performance and craftsmanship of the Premium SR Series. Finished in a striking red, white, and blue design, this commemorative instrument delivers the fast playability, powerful tone, and premium appointments that have made the SR line a favorite among bassists worldwide.

At its core is a resonant African mahogany body paired with a five-piece panga panga and purpleheart Atlas neck reinforced with KTS titanium rods for outstanding stability, sustain, and long-term reliability. A pair of Nordstrand™ Big Break pickups provides punchy lows, articulate mids, and crisp highs, while the Ibanez Custom 3-band EQ, EQ bypass switch, and 3-way mid-frequency selector offer an impressive range of tonal possibilities for virtually any musical style.

Warm, Resonant Tone

The African mahogany body delivers a rich, balanced voice with excellent sustain and clarity. Its warm tonal character provides a solid low-end foundation while maintaining definition across the frequency spectrum. The ergonomically contoured SR body is lightweight and exceptionally comfortable, making long rehearsals and performances effortless.

Fast, Stable Neck

Designed for speed and precision, the five-piece Atlas neck combines panga panga and purpleheart for exceptional strength and feel. Internal KTS titanium reinforcement rods enhance rigidity while improving sustain and note definition. The slim SR profile, bound panga panga fingerboard, premium fret edge treatment, and 24 medium frets provide a smooth, fast-playing experience that's equally at home on stage or in the studio.

Premium Electronics

Nordstrand Big Break pickups feature a split-coil design housed in a soapbar casing, delivering the clarity and articulation of single coils with the punch and noise-free performance of humbuckers. The Ibanez Custom 3-band EQ offers precise control over bass, midrange, and treble frequencies, while the 3-way mid-frequency switch allows you to fine-tune your sound. Flip the EQ bypass switch for passive operation and a more traditional bass response whenever you need it.

Professional Hardware

The MR5S bridge provides precise intonation adjustment and adjustable string spacing for a customized playing feel. Gotoh tuning machines ensure dependable tuning stability, while the Graph Tech Black TUSQ XL nut promotes smooth string movement and consistent performance.

A Patriotic Limited Edition

Exclusive red, white, and blue graphics, a matching headstock, and elegant acrylic block inlays give the SR4A250 a bold visual identity worthy of its 250th Anniversary celebration. Whether you're adding it to your collection or taking it on stage, this limited-edition model offers premium tone, exceptional playability, and unmistakable style.

Features

  • Limited-edition SR bass celebrates the 250th anniversary of the United States
  • Bold red, white and blue finish with matching headstock delivers bold, celebratory style
  • African mahogany body provides warmth, clarity and strong sustain
  • 5-piece panga panga/purpleheart Atlas neck enhances stability and resonance
  • KTS titanium rods reinforce neck strength and improve sustain
  • Bound panga panga fingerboard provides tight lows and articulate attack
  • 24 medium frets with premium fret edge treatment ensures smooth playability
  • Nordstrand Big Break split-coil pickups deliver punchy, articulate tone
  • Ibanez Custom 3-band EQ, 3-way mid frequency switch and EQ bypass switch ensure total tonal control
  • MR5S bridge allows adjustable string spacing and accurate intonation
  • Gotoh tuners and Graph Tech black TUSQ XL nut ensure tuning stability
  • Gig bag included for secure transport to gigs and studio sessions

General

  • Product type: Electric bass guitar
  • Series: SR
  • Model: SR4A250250th Anniversary

Body

item.title: Carvin SB4000 Jazz Bass 2011 Mahogany — $1,100
item.description:
$1,100 · Excellent · Tom Lawrence

Carvin SB4000 Jazz Bass, Mahogany / Ash, Made in USA, with OHSC

This is a very nice Carvin SB4000, designed with Sekou Bunch in 2009, it was released in 2010 and ran until around 2013 when the JB4 was created as Carvin rebranded into Kiesel again for their top end guitar line. This bass has many custom features including the mid-sweep stacked pot, a matching mahogany headstock, and beautiful pearl block inlays on a rosewood fingerboard.

The SB4000 features a tulip shaped flare to the classic Jazz bass shape and the pickups are set back in the vintage style of the older fenders. There are two screw holes where the original ash tray pickup cover was removed. I do not have it, but they can be ordered if you are really into keeping with the late 60's / 70's looks. 

This bass is in great condition, just a few marks on the headstock and on the bottom by the input jack, they are well pictured. The neck is straight, the frets are in excellent shape.  All the electronics are good and the 18v preamp is HOT!  It plays like a dream, but I already have four fretted four-strings, and I need to move one out.

It comes with the original hard case that is branded with the Carvin logo.  I put Dunlop Strap-Locks on it and will also include a Levy’s leather strap and a pack of fresh 9V batteries.  This bass has been for in home use only and has never seen cigarette smoke. 

It’s a USA made work of art.


Carvin’s old literature says: 
Carvin’s SB Series basses feature entirely new bolt on necks. The SB4000 is based on a traditional jazz style neck. The rounded neck heel allows comfortable playability to the last fret and its retro-style body features front and rear contours for hours of comfortable playing.

NEW J99A Pickups - The SB series basses come equipped with a pair of J99A single coil pickups which feature Alnico V magnets. These pickups deliver a rich bell-tone with full bottom end, clear upper range and the right amount of mid-range growl.

NEW Optional Active Electronics
• 18 volt active electronics
• Separate Volume controls for each pickup
• Mid-Sweep stacked option
• Stacked bass/treble cut-boost controls
• Side mounted input jack

Bass Player Online Says: 
Carvin has always been on the map for unique high-end instruments at low prices, but these new basses—designed in conjunction with monster L.A. bassist Sekou Bunch—are a surprisingly rootsy departure for the direct-sale-only brand. The SB4000/5000 series is a bold attempt to bridge retro J-style bass design with modern electronics and hardware. Not only are the SB basses another step in Carvin’s evolution as instrument makers, the impressive list of customizable options puts a ton of control into the hands of prospective buyers.

One does not look at these basses and say “Carvin.” They’ve left behind their other more pointy designs for something with some classy Old School personality. Some may disagree, but I think the bass’s tulip shape is a fresh new take on the J-Bass tradition. The clear-coated wood grain, heavy die-cast bridge, and inlays were simply gorgeous.

The SB’s bolt-on neck is a departure for Carvin. The vast majority of Carvin’s catalog is neck-through. The sculpted neck joint was well designed and allowed unobstructed access to the higher frets. Both instruments came to me set up with spot-on intonation and appropriate action (higher for the 4 and a little lower for the 5). I generally have to tinker with set-up with a new instrument and was impressed to find myself with nothing to do with the SB’s. The tung-oiled necks were silky smooth and a pleasure to play, with a shallow-C profile.

Another new step for Carvin are the SB’s updated electronics. With Sekou’s encouragement, Carvin designed a whole new system, adding J-style volume/volume controls, instead of a master volume with blend arrangement, and including a passive tone control. For the active section, the stacked cut/boost bass and treble filter were useable throughout their range. I didn’t spend much time fishing for good tone.

The pickups themselves are also newly designed for this bass—Alnico V single-coils that pop, purr, and whisper when necessary. The neck-position tones are warm and woody, while the bridge pickups have a nice honky sizzle to cut above the band for soloing. It’s not enough to offer pretty, resonant wood if the electronics can’t keep up, but Carvin obviously put a lot of time into this detail. I had no problem copping a big palette of genre-specific tones, from tubby low-end R&B to bright slap slice. The basses also sounded fabulous in my home-studio, yielding tons of personality and balance. The tone controls were responsive and behaved predictably. I think this is a great testament to well-engineered electronics, and a requirement for any working musician.

Carvin designed these basses for players at any level of expertise, and I think they’ve certainly hit their mark. In particular, the 4-string does what a 4-string jazz bass should do: It’s meaty, with a wide palette of easily manipulated tones. I dug the beefy neck contour and the sleek ebony fingerboard, which tapers nicely down to the nut giving it a classic Fender-ish feel. Kudos to Carvin for ushering in a whole new generation of hip, modern, and affordable instruments.

_________________________________________________________________________
Brand: Carvin / Kiesel
Year: 2011-Ish (Carvin does not catalog their SNs)
Model: SB4000
Body: Mahogany top on a Swamp Ash body
Neck: 5-piece maple with 22 frets, pearl block inlays
Hardware: Carvin branded tuners 20:1, Hipshot bridge
Pickups: 2X J99A (Brown Dot) Alnico V Single Coil pickups
Controls: 2x volume with 3 band EQ (Bass, Treble, Mid sweep and cut / boost)

The Squier Sonic Precision Bass is ready to launch any musical adventure into warp speed, offering iconic Fender style and inspiring tone for players at any stage. 

This P Bass sports a slim and inviting "C"-shaped neck profile with a narrow 1.5" nut width and a thin, lightweight body for optimal playing comfort while a Squier split single-coil pickup delivers punchy bass tone.

item.title: Fender Squier Sonic Bronco Bass with Laurel Fingerboard (Daphne Blue) — $259.99
item.description:
$259.99 · Mint · Focus Pro Audio

The Squier Sonic Bronco Bass is designed to deliver classic style, comfortable playability, and inspiring tone in a compact short-scale format. Ideal for beginners and experienced players alike, this bass features a slim neck profile and a lightweight body that make it easy to play for extended sessions. A responsive single-coil pickup provides clear and punchy low-end sound, while dependable hardware ensures tuning stability and accurate intonation. Built for comfort, control, and versatility, this bass is ready for practice, recording, or live performance.

Short 30-Inch Scale Length

The shorter scale length offers reduced string tension, making fretting easier and improving overall comfort, especially for players with smaller hands or those transitioning from guitar.

Slim C-Shaped Neck Profile

A smooth and inviting neck shape allows for fast movement along the fingerboard and a comfortable grip during long playing sessions.

Single-Coil Bass Pickup

The ceramic single-coil pickup delivers a focused, punchy bass tone with clear articulation suitable for a wide range of musical styles.

Lightweight Poplar Body

The thin, lightweight body helps reduce player fatigue while maintaining balanced resonance and classic bass feel.

4-Saddle Hardtail Bridge

The adjustable bridge allows precise intonation and string height adjustments for consistent tuning accuracy and performance reliability.

Sealed-Gear Tuning Machines

Die-cast sealed tuners provide smooth operation and dependable tuning stability during practice and live use.

Chrome-Finished Hardware

Durable hardware adds long-lasting reliability and a clean, professional appearance.

item.description:
$857.92 · Excellent · Tommy's Gear Emporium

Squier Vintage Modified Bass VI from 2015, the most respected Squier Bass VI version, better than modern Classic Vibes. Made at well respected Cort factory in Indonesia.


Bass has been repainted metallic golden and looks stunning. The painting work quality is excellent. The original bridge has been replaced with Halon bridge which is a huge improvement to the original. it helps tunng stability a lot and helps with sustain. Unfortunately tremolo arm not available. Fender 24-100 strings on.


Everything works flawlessly. Neck is straight. Frets in good condition. No noise from electronics. Truss rod works normally in both directions. Easy to set up to make it playable, intonates well. The bridge has bushings which keeps the bridge from rocking.


Free shipping inside EU.

Will be shipped well packed to prevent any shipping damage.


Send me a question if you have something you need to know!


Unfortunately no returns accepted. Buy with confidence!

Description


The Classic Vibe ’60s Jazz Bass® pays tribute to the iconic decade that defined the Jazz Bass legacy, combining vintage-inspired styling with modern playability. Designed by Fender®, it delivers the signature articulate, punchy tone that made the original models a staple across genres, from soul and funk to rock and beyond.

Equipped with dual Fender-Designed alnico single-coil pickups, this bass offers a wide tonal palette with rich clarity and dynamic response. A slim “C”-shaped neck profile and 9.5" radius fingerboard provide a comfortable, fast-playing feel, while the vintage-tint gloss neck finish and nickel-plated hardware complete its authentic old-school aesthetic.
